text: Artificial intelligence is changing many aspects of our lives, but it also raises important ethical questions. One major concern is bias. If the data used to train AI systems contains unfair patterns, the AI can make biased decisions, affecting people's jobs, loans, or even legal outcomes. Fairness requires that these systems treat everyone equally, regardless of their background. Privacy is another key issue. AI often uses personal information, so we must protect this data and respect people's rights. Transparency means that companies should explain how their AI works and why it makes certain choices. Finally, responsible AI development involves creating systems that are safe, accountable, and beneficial for all. Everyone involved must carefully consider these ethical principles.
